official_name: Forum of Incident Response and Security Teams
notes: FIRST coordinates incident response globally. Publishes CVSS (Common Vulnerability Scoring System) and EPSS (Exploit Prediction Scoring System), among other standards and best practices for incident response and security teams.

data.description: Industry standard for scoring the severity of security vulnerabilities. Provides Base, Temporal/Threat, and Environmental metric groups producing a score from 0.0 to 10.0.
data.notes:
CVSS v4.0 is the current version (released November 2023). CVSS v3.1 remains widely used across the industry. Each version has a specification document, user guide, and online calculator. CVSS is maintained by the CVSS Special Interest Group (SIG) within FIRST.

CVSS is a specification/standard, not a database of identifiers — it defines how to score vulnerabilities, not individual vulnerability records. Filed under reference because it is a document/specification that other systems reference. Companion entry: secid:methodology/first.org/cvss covers the scoring methodology (assessment guidance, metric definitions) that goes beyond the format.

versions_available.note: Current version. Adds Supplemental metric group, renames Temporal to Threat.
Still widely used. Minor revision of 3.0 with improved guidance.
Legacy. Still seen in older vulnerability databases.

urls.note: CVSS JSON and XML schema representations

children.description: CVSS v3.0 specification document (released 2015; superseded by 3.1).
CVSS v2 complete guide. There is no separate 'specification document' for v2; the complete guide at /cvss/v2/guide is the canonical document.
CVSS v1 landing page. No separate specification document survives on first.org — the v1 area only has the version's overview and the introduction paper. Resolves to the landing page.

examples.note: CVSS v1 landing page (historical; no separate spec doc)

data.description: Data-driven model for estimating the probability that a vulnerability will be exploited in the wild within 30 days.
data.notes: Data-driven model for estimating the probability that a vulnerability will be exploited in the wild within 30 days. Scores range 0-1 (probability). Updated daily. Complements CVSS severity with exploitation likelihood.
